name: Bastides of Bassoues and Marciac Loop
name_original: Bastides de Bassoues et Marciac – boucle par la vallée du Midour
description: Cycle a moderate 19.2-mile loop through the Midour valley, exploring historic bastides like Bassoues and Marciac in the Gers region.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Bassoues offers routes through the rolling hills and agricultural landscapes of Gascony, France. The region is characterized by its vineyards, sunflower fields, and historic bastide towns, providing a scenic backdrop for cycling. Routes often follow quiet country roads, connecting villages and offering views of the rural French countryside. The terrain typically features gentle ascents and descents, suitable for various fitness levels. Best touring cycling routes around Bassoues The most popular touring cycling route is Bastides of Bassoues and Marciac Loop, a 19.2 miles (30.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 0 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects historic bastide towns with scenic countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Lupiac – Lupiac loop from Scieurac-et-Flourès, a difficult 51.9 miles (83.5 km) path. This longer route explores the wider region, passing through varied rural landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Lupiac loop from Bassoues, a 27.7 miles (44.6 km) trail leading through the picturesque area around Lupiac, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Touring cycling around Bassoues is defined by rolling hills, vineyards, and historic bastide towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more advanced long-distance bike routes. The routes in Bassoues are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bassoues's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Bassoues?
A: The touring cycling routes around Bassoues primarily feature rolling hills, making for a varied terrain. You'll find a good mix of options, with 19 moderate routes and 15 difficult routes available. There is also 1 easy route for those seeking a gentler ride.

Q: Are there any longer touring bike routes for experienced cyclists in the Bassoues region?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists looking for a challenge, there are several longer routes. One notable difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41548235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lupiac – Lupiac loop from Scieurac-et-Flourès&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 51.9 miles (83.5 km) and features significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect while touring cycling near Bassoues?
A: The region offers picturesque Gascon landscapes, characterized by vineyards, vast sunflower fields, and historic bastide towns. Routes often follow quiet country roads, providing expansive views of the rural French countryside and connecting charming villages.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes suitable for beginners around Bassoues?
A: While many routes involve rolling hills, there is at least one easy route available. For families or beginners, it's best to look for shorter, flatter sections or routes specifically marked as easy. The overall terrain is generally suitable for various fitness levels, but some routes are more challenging than others.

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in Bassoues?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for touring cycling in Bassoues, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery as the vineyards change color or the sunflowers bloom. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any circular touring bike routes available near Bassoues?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021123595/bastides-of-bassoues-and-marciac-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastides of Bassoues and Marciac Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which connects historic towns and offers a scenic return to your starting point.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: You can explore several historic and natural attractions. The routes often pass through or near charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6364610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marciac&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3553918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lupiac&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2700350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide de Bassoues&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Our Lady of the Assumption of Marciac&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, there are beautiful lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5856460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac du Lizet&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Bassoues?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet country roads, the picturesque landscapes of vineyards and sunflower fields, and the charm of the historic bastide towns encountered along the routes.

Q: Are there any moderate-difficulty routes that explore the wider region around Bassoues?
A: Certainly. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39962371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lupiac loop from Bassoues&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 27.7 miles (44.6 km) trail that takes you through the picturesque area around Lupiac, offering a good balance of distance and challenge.

Q: Where can I find parking when starting a touring bike route in Bassoues?
A: Many of the routes either start directly from Bassoues or nearby villages. You can typically find public parking in the centers of these towns. For specific routes, checking the komoot tour details will often provide information on recommended starting points and parking availability.

Q: Are there any difficult touring routes that offer a different perspective of the region?
A: Yes, for a more challenging ride that explores a different part of the region, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6980776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide de Bassoues – Marciac loop from Marciac&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 47.5 miles (76.5 km) route provides extensive views and a good workout.

Q: What is the typical duration for a touring cycling route in Bassoues?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15013745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide de Bassoues – Bastide of Bassoues loop from Marciac&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 18.4 miles (29.6 km), typically takes around 2 hours. Longer, more difficult routes can take 5 hours or more.
tips: The bastide of Bassoues (pronounced Bassous) is a fortified village in the Gers with a keep forty meters high which attests to the existence of the fortress of the bishops of Auch. Built on top of a hill, the city can be seen from afar.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The Bastides of Bassoues and Marciac – loop via the Midour valley is designed for touring bicycles and generally takes about 2 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is largely paved, with approximately 26.4 kilometers of asphalt and 4.16 kilometers of tarred surfaces. However, be aware that some segments may be unpaved and more challenging to navigate. Overall, it's a moderate ride requiring good fitness.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through charming landscapes characteristic of Southwest France, including verdant valleys, vineyards, and sunflower fields. Key highlights include the historic bastides of Bastide de Bassoues, known for its hilltop location and 40-meter high keep, and the settlement of Marciac. The route also follows the tranquil Midour valley.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This loop is classified as a 'moderate bike ride' and requires a good level of fitness. While much of it is paved, some unpaved sections and the overall elevation gain of 430 meters might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, both Bassoues and Marciac are good starting points for this loop. You can typically find parking within these bastide towns to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The region is known for its sunflower fields and vineyards, suggesting that late spring through early autumn would offer the most picturesque scenery and pleasant cycling weather. Summer can be warm, so consider early morning rides.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a touring bicycle route on public roads and paths, dogs are generally permitted, especially if kept on a leash. However, always be mindful of local regulations and other cyclists.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public loop through the Bastides of Bassoues and Marciac.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the PR7 :Circuit des coteaux, Chemin des Remparts du Nord, Place de la Halle, and Passage de la Tourette.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The bastide towns of Bassoues and Marciac offer opportunities to experience local cuisine and traditional markets, making them good spots to refuel or enjoy a break during your ride.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this touring bicycle trip?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and potential for unpaved sections, we recommend bringing a repair kit, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and appropriate cycling attire. A map or GPS device is also advisable.
